findings_fixed.description: Wrapped mcp package imports in mcp_gateway.py with try/except ImportError, setting HAS_MCP sentinel. Added _require_mcp() helper that raises RuntimeError with install instructions when MCP features are used without the package. Called _require_mcp() at the start of connect_url() and connect_command(). The from __future__ import annotations ensures ClientSession type annotation in MCPConnection dataclass is evaluated lazily. roots/__init__.py import of MCPGateway is now safe without mcp installed since mcp_gateway.py handles the missing import gracefully.

Added _validate_command() function to mcp_gateway.py that rejects empty commands, executables containing path traversal (..), and executables containing shell metacharacters (;|&$`'\!#~<>{}()*?[]). Called at the start of connect_command() before any subprocess is spawned. Note: a full executable allowlist would require additional design decisions about permitted MCP server binaries and is recommended as a follow-up.

findings_unfixable.reason: Adding API authentication requires architectural decisions about the auth model (API keys, JWT, OAuth, etc.), key storage, and middleware design that are beyond a targeted fix. This should be addressed as a separate feature spec with its own design review. The CORS middleware also allows all origins which compounds the issue but is similarly an architectural concern.
